hook: You already know how to describe a single random variable with a distribution — but the real world rarely gives you just one variable to worry about. When two quantities are linked, like the heights of two related individuals or the joint losses in a financial portfolio, a single distribution isn't enough to capture the full picture. How do we model relationships between random variables, and what happens when those models meet real-world risk?
concept: This unit extends your probabilistic toolkit from single variables to interacting systems, connecting the mathematics of joint distributions to the practical language of risk — unified by the idea of *multivariate probability models and their applications*.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to model and interpret the joint behavior of two correlated normal random variables, including computing marginal and conditional distributions using the correlation parameter $\rho$.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Introduction to Risk and Insurance Models
topic_roadmap.description: Apply probability distributions to model uncertain losses, calculate expected claims, and understand how insurers use these tools to price risk and manage financial exposure.
